data_type_evidence: Real benchmark questions are loaded from Hugging Face datasets in `mlrbench/MLRagent_tasks_youra_result_sonnet45_experiment/iclr2025_buildingtrust/claude_code/data_processing.py:24` (`load_dataset('trivia_qa', ...)`) and `mlrbench/MLRagent_tasks_youra_result_sonnet45_experiment/iclr2025_buildingtrust/claude_code/data_processing.py:55` (`load_dataset('commonsense_qa', ...)`).

The actual experiment runner uses simulated model outputs rather than real LLM API calls: `mlrbench/MLRagent_tasks_youra_result_sonnet45_experiment/iclr2025_buildingtrust/claude_code/run_experiment_mock.py:18` imports `MockModelSystem`, `.../run_experiment_mock.py:82` instantiates it, and `.../run_experiment_mock.py:99` logs `Collected mock responses`.

Execution artifacts confirm the run used mock responses: `mlrbench/MLRagent_tasks_youra_result_sonnet45_experiment/iclr2025_buildingtrust/results/log.txt:2` says `ADAPTIVE CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION EXPERIMENT (MOCK MODELS)`, and lines 17, 23, and 29 report collected mock responses for train/val/test.

Synthetic fallback dataset creators also exist in `mlrbench/MLRagent_tasks_youra_result_sonnet45_experiment/iclr2025_buildingtrust/claude_code/data_processing.py:93` and `.../data_processing.py:122`, reinforcing that this codebase explicitly supports artificial data generation.
